old_claim.note: Global Positioning System (GPS) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct GPS dependency remains because coordinated multi-satellite systems such as Transit were operating before the 1973 NAVSTAR GPS program, making GPS a later constellation application rather than a prerequisite for constellations.

source_shape.source_locator: Transit section: dates the first Transit satellite to 1960, initial operational capability to 1964, and describes a three-satellite operational navigation system predating NAVSTAR GPS.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The official navigation-systems history documents an operational multi-satellite navigation system years before the NAVSTAR GPS program was approved.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A technology cannot be a causal prerequisite for a system class already demonstrated before that technology's own scoped milestone; GPS is one later use of constellation architecture.

ontology_before: Satellite constellations were modeled as depending on the later GPS program.
ontology_after: Satellite constellations retain their pre-NAVSTAR chronology, while GPS remains a downstream satellite-navigation architecture.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-gps-navstar-logistics-boundary: satellite_constellations has no direct gps prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped specifically to the NAVSTAR operational constellation rather than the general coordinated multi-satellite system class.
Authoritative chronology establishes that no coordinated satellite system existed before NAVSTAR GPS.
short_reason: Operational satellite constellations predated NAVSTAR, so the GPS-to-constellation arrow was chronologically reversed.
